title: The One Where Nana Dies Twice

lines.text: [Scene: Chandler's Office, Chandler is on a coffee break.   Shelley enters.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Hey gorgeous, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dehydrated Japanese noodles under fluorescent lights... does it get better than this?

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Question. You're not dating anybody, are you, because I met somebody who would be perfect for you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, y'see, perfect might be a problem. Had you said 'co-dependent', or 'self-destructive'...

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Do you want a date Saturday?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Okay. He's cute, he's funny, he's-

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's a he?

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Well yeah! ...Oh God. I- just- I thought- Good, Shelley. I'm just gonna go flush myself down the toilet now...(backs out of the room) Okay, goodbye...
Opening Credits
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...Couldn't enjoy a cup of noodles after that. I mean, is that ridiculous? Can you believe she actually thought that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um... yeah. Well, I mean, when I first met you, y'know, I thought maybe, possibly, you might be...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You did?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but then you spent Phoebe's entire birthday party talking to my breasts, so then I figured maybe not.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh. Did, uh... any of the rest of you guys think that when you first met me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I did.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I think so, yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nono, me neither. Although, uh, y'know, back in college, Susan Sallidor did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're kidding! Did you tell her I wasn't?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. No, it's just 'cause, uh, I kinda wanted to go out with her too, so I told her, actually, you were seeing Bernie Spellman... who also liked her, so...
Joey congratulates Ross, sees Chandler's look and abruptly stops.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, this is fascinating. So, uh, what is it about me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I dunno, 'cause you're smart, you're funny...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross is smart and funny, d'you ever think that about him?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! Right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: WHAT IS IT?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I-I d'know, you-you just- you have a quality.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yes. Absolutely. A quality.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, oh, a quality, good, because I was worried you guys were gonna be vague about this.
Phone rings; Monica gets it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? Hello? Oh! Rachel, it's Paolo calling from Rome.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Calling from Rome! (Takes phone) Bon giorno, caro mio.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey) So he's calling from Rome. I could do that. Just gotta go to Rome.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, your dad just beeped in, but can you make it quick? Talking to Rome. (Showing off to Phoebe and Chandler) I'm talking to Rome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey dad, what's up? (Listens) Oh God. Ross, it's Nana.
[Scene: The Hospital, Mr. and Mrs. Geller are there, along with Aunt Lillian. Ross and Monica enter and everyone says hi and kisses.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, how's she doing?

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: The doctor says it's a matter of hours.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How-how are you, Mom?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Me? I'm fine, fine. I'm glad you're here. ...What's with your hair?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: What's different?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh, maybe that's it.
Monica strides over to Ross, who is making coffee, and talks to him aside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She is unbelievable, our mother is...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, relax, relax. We are gonna be here for a while, it looks like, and we still have boyfriends and your career to cover.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!
They hug.
[Cut to the hospital, later. Everyone is talking about Nana.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The fuzzy little mints at the bottom of her purse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! ...Yeah, they were gross. Oh, you know what I loved? Her Sweet 'n' Los. How she was always stealing them from- from restaurants.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Not just restaurants, from our house.
The nurse comes out of Nana's room.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Mrs. Geller?
Everyone stands up. Cut to Ross and Monica in Nana's room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She looks so small.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, at least she's with Pop-Pop and Aunt Phyllis now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: G'bye, Nana. (She kisses her on the forehead.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye, Nana.
He goes to kiss her but she moves. Monica screams. Ross shouts and stares in disbelief. Monica runs out of the room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross!
Ross runs out too.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: What is going on?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know how-how the nurse said that-that Nana had passed? Well, she's not, quite..

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She's not- past, she's present, she's back.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: (reentering) What's going on?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: She may have died.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: She may have died?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: We're looking into it.
Monica returns with the nurse and they go into Nana's room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I, uh, I'll go see. (He goes in)

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: This almost never happens!
Nana passes for the second time and the nurse pulls the blanket over her. Ross and Monica go to tell the family

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now she's passed.
Central Perk, Chandler, Phoebe, Joey, and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just have to know, okay. Is it my hair?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (exasperated) Yes, Chandler, that's exactly what it is. It's your hair.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you have homosexual hair.
Monica and Ross enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, um, did she...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Twice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Twice?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, that sucks!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I dunno, it's weird. I mean, I know she's gone, but I just don't feel, uh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe that's 'cause she's not really gone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nono, she's gone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We checked. A lot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm, I mean maybe no-one ever really goes. Ever since my mom died, every now and then, I get the feeling that she's like right here, y'know? (She circles her hand around her right shoulder. Chandler, sitting on her right, draws back nervously) Oh! And Debbie, my best friend from junior high- got struck by lightning on a miniature golf course- I always get this really strong Debbie vibe whenever I use one of those little yellow pencils, y'know? ...I miss her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw. Hey, Pheebs, want this? (Gives her a pencil)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure. I just sharpened her this morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, see, I don't believe any of that. I think once you're dead, you're dead! You're gone! You're worm food! (realises his tactlessness) ...So Chandler looks gay, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, I dunno who this is, but it's not Debbie. (Hands back the pencil)
Nana's house, Ross, Mrs. Geller and Aunt Lillian are going through clothes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I thought it was gonna be a closed casket.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Well, that doesn't mean she can't look nice!
They open a cupboard which, amongst other things, contains a chest of drawers

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Sweetie, you think you can get in there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) I don't see why not.
He tries pushing against the chest of drawers. Then he opens one of the drawers and climbs into the closet using that; he falls behind the chest of drawers with a shout.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here's my retainer!
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is talking to her father.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I was just thinking. When my time comes-

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Dad!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Listen to me! When my time comes, I wanna be buried at sea.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You what?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I wanna be buried at sea, it looks like fun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Define fun.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: C'mon, you'll make a day of it! You'll rent a boat, pack a lunch...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...And then we throw your body in the water... Gee, that does sound fun.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Everyone thinks they know me. Everyone says 'Jack Geller, so predictable'. Maybe after I'm gone, they'll say 'Buried at sea! Huh!'.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's probably what they'll say.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I'd like that.
Chandler's Office, Shelley is drinking coffee; Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, gorgeous.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: (sheepish) Hey. Look, I'm sorry about yesterday, I, um-

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, nono, don't- don't worry about it. Believe me, apparently other people have made the same mistake.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Oh! Okay! Phew!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, uh... what do you think it is about me?

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: I dunno, uh... you just have a-a...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...Quality, right, great.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Y'know, it's a shame, because you and Lowell would've made a great couple.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Lowell? Financial Services' Lowell, that's who you saw me with?

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: What? He's cute!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, yeah... 's'no Brian in Payroll.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Is Brian...?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Uh, I d'know! The point is, if you were gonna set me up with someone, I'd like to think you'd set me up with someone like him.

lines.speaker: Shelley
lines.text: Well, I think Brian's a little out of your league.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me? You don't think I could get a Brian? Because I could get a Brian. Believe you me. ...I'm really not.
Nana's Bedroom, Ross is holding a dress out from inside the closet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (holding a dress out from inside the closet) This one?

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have shown you everything we have. Unless you want your mother to spend eternity in a lemon yellow pant-suit, go with the burgundy.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: You know, whatever we pick, she would've told us it's the wrong one.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You're right. We'll go with the burgundy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! A fine choice. I'm coming out. (Starts to climb over the furniture)

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: Wait! We need shoes!
Ross falls back inside

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Um, how about these? (Holds out a pair)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: That's really a day shoe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And where she's going everyone else'll be dressier?

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: Could we see something in a slimmer heel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (forages around) Okay, I have nothing in an evening shoe in the burgundy. I can show you something in a silver that may work.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lillian
lines.text: No, it really should be burgundy.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Mm. Unless we go with a different dress?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Nonono, wait a sec. I may have something in the back.
He finds a shoebox (out of shot), pulls it down and opens it. It is full of Sweet 'n' Lo's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God..

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Is everything all right, dear?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, just... just Nana stuff.
He reaches up higher and knocks down another shoebox lid. Sweet 'n' Lo's rain down on him
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are preparing to leave for the funeral.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) How we doing, you guys ready?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mom already called this morning to remind me not to wear my hair up. Did you know my ears are not my best feature?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Some days it's all I can think about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hi, sorry I'm late, I couldn't find my bearings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you-you mean your earrings?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What'd I say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sticking her foot out) Hm-m.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are these the shoes?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. Paolo sent them from Italy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, we-uh- we don't have shoes here, or...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering with Chandler) Morning. We ready to go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, don't we look nice all dressed up?...It's stuff like that, isn't it?
They all leave.
The cemetary, after the funeral.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was a really beautiful service.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: It really was. Oh, c'mere, sweetheart. (Hugs her) Y'know, I think it might be time for you to start using night cream.
Joey listens to his overcoat for a second and sighs, then notices Chandler watching

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing, just your overcoat sounds remarkably like Brent Mussberger.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check it out, Giants-Cowboys. (He has a pocket TV)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're watching a football game at a funeral?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it's the pre-game. I'm gonna watch it at the reception.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are a frightening, frightening man.
Rachel steps in a patch of mud

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no! My new Paolo shoes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I hope they're not ruined.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, what a great day. ...What? Weather-wise!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, uh, the air, the-the trees... even though Nana's gone there's, there's something almost, uh- I dunno, almost life-aff- (Not looking where he is going he falls into an open grave)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: God! Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm fine. Just-just... having my worst fear realised...
The Wake, at the Gellers' house. Ross is lying on his back, with Phoebe squatting over him, checking to see if he's injured.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, don't worry, I'm just checking to see if the muscle's in spasm...huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You missed a belt loop.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! No-n-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, it's in spasm.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Here, sweetie, here. I took these when I had my golfing accident. (Hands Ross a bottle of pills. Then turns to Monica and pats her hair over her ears)
Cut to Chandler and a woman, Andrea, reaching for the same slice of meat

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, no-

lines.speaker: Andrea
lines.text: Sorry- Hi, I'm Dorothy's daughter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, I'm Chandler, and I have no idea who Dorothy is.
They shake hands. Cut to Ross emerging from a hallway, grinning inanely. He is obviously very stoned

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, look who's up! How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I feel great. I feel- great, I fleel great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, those pills really worked, huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not the first two, but the second two- woooo! ...I love you guys. You guys are the greatest. I love my sister (Kisses Monica), I love Pheebs... (Hugs her)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! That's so nice...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (hugs him) And listen, man, if you wanna be gay, be gay. Doesn't matter to me.

lines.speaker: Andrea
lines.text: (turns to a friend) You were right. (They walk off and leave Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel. Rachel Rachel. (Sits down beside her) I love you the most.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (humouring him) Oh, well you know who I love the most?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.. you don't get it! (Passes out and slumps across her)
Cut to Joey watching TV in the corner. He makes an extravagant gesture of disappointment.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Whaddya got there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (hides the TV, but he still has an earphone) Just a, uh... hearing disability.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: What's the score?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seventeen-fourteen Giants... three minutes to go in the third.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Beautiful! (Turns to watch with him)
Time lapse. A large crowd of men are now watching the game

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (still trapped under Ross) Pheebs, could you maybe hand me a cracker?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (to Monica) Your grandmother would have hated this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, sure, what with it being her funeral and all.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: No, I'd be hearing about 'Why didn't I get the honey-glazed ham?', I didn't spend enough on flowers, and if I spent more she'd be saying 'Why are you wasting your money? I don't need flowers, I'm dead'.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That sounds like Nana.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Do you know what it's like to grow up with someone who is critical of every single thing you say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...I can imagine.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I'm telling you, it's a wonder your mother turned out to be the positive, life-affirming person that she is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is a wonder. So tell me something, Mom. If you had to do it all over again, I mean, if she was here right now, would you tell her?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Tell her what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How she drove you crazy, picking on every little detail, like your hair... for example.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I'm not sure I know what you're getting at.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you think things would have been better if you'd just told her the truth?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: ...No. I think some things are better left unsaid. I think it's nicer when people just get along.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: More wine, dear?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I think so.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (reaches out to fiddle with Monica's hair again, and realises) Those earrings look really lovely on you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. They're yours.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Actually they were Nana's.
There is a cry of disappointment from the crowd of men.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Now I'm depressed! ...(To everyone) Even more than I was.
Central Perk, the gang are looking at old photos.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, who's this little naked guy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That little naked guy would be me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aww, look at the little thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, yes, fine, that is my penis. Can we be grown-ups now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who are those people?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Got me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's Nana, right there in the middle. (Reads the back) 'Me and the gang at Java Joe's'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, Monica, you look just like your grandmother. How old was she there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let's see, 1939... yeah, 24, 25?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Looks like a fun gang. (They all look at each other and smile)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, look-look-look-look-look! I got Monica naked!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking) Nono, that would be me again. I'm, uh, just trying something.
Closing Credits
Chandler's Office, Chandler is on a coffee break as Lowell enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, Lowell.

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: Hey, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So how's it going there in Financial Services?

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: It's like Mardi Gras without the paper mache heads. How 'bout you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good, good. Listen, heh, I dunno what Shelley told you about me, but, uh... I'm not.

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: I know. That's what I told her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So- you can tell?

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: Pretty much, most of the time. We have a kind of... radar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you don't think I have a, a quality?

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: Speaking for my people, I'd have to say no. By the way, your friend Brian from Payroll, he is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He is?

lines.speaker: Lowell
lines.text: Yup, and waaay out of your league. (Exits)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Out of my league. I could get a Brian. (Brian enters behind him) If I wanted to get a Brian, I could get a Brian. (Sees him) Hey, Brian.
End
